sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I understand level 2:
- Wall (G) is on the right, starting at col 8
- 4 blue blocks on the left (cols 0-3) at rows 16-19, 24-27, 36-39, 44-47
- Upper black bar at rows 20-23 (cols 0-55)
- Lower black bar at rows 40-43 with a green (N) gap at cols 28-29
- Figure (c) at rows 52-57, cols 10-13, with green (N) strip at cols 8-9
- White (W) regions separate the blue blocks
The wall starts at col 8 (56 columns wide from col 8 to 63). The figure is embedded in the wall at cols 10-13.
In level 1, the goal was to expand the wall to fill the entire grid by clicking the lower blue block 3 times. Let me think about what the equivalent is here.
The wall is 56 columns wide (cols 8-63). To fill the entire 64-column grid, I need to expand it by 8 more columns (to col 0). If each blue click expands by 4 columns, I need 2 clicks.
But there are 4 blue blocks, not 2. Let me think about which ones to click.
Looking at the layout:
- Blue block 1: rows 16-19 (above upper black bar)
- Blue block 2: rows 24-27 (between bars, left of wall)
- Blue block 3: rows 36-39 (between bars, left of wall)
- Blue block 4: rows 44-47 (below lower black bar, left of wall)
In level 1, the two blue blocks were:
- Upper blue (rows 25-28): moved figure right/shrunk wall
- Lower blue (rows 33-36): moved figure left/expanded wall
In level 2, the wall is on the right. The "expand wall" direction would be to the left (toward col 0). Let me think about which blue blocks correspond to which actions.
Looking at the white regions:
- Above upper bar (rows 1-19): white, cols 0-51
- Between bars (rows 24-39): white cols 0-11, then wall
- Below lower bar (rows 44-63): white cols 0-7, then wall
The blue blocks at rows 24-27 and 36-39 are in the region between the bars, where the wall starts at col 12. The blue blocks at rows 44-47 are below the lower bar, where the wall starts at col 8.
I think the 4 blue blocks might have different effects. Let me try clicking them to see what happens. Given the efficiency budget, I should try the most likely candidate first.
In level 1, clicking the lower blue (closer to the figure) expanded the wall. In level 2, the figure is at rows 52-57, so the closest blue block is at rows 44-47. Let me try clicking that one.
Actually, looking more carefully at level 1:
- Upper blue was at rows 25-28 (above the black bar at rows 30-33)
- Lower blue was at rows 33-36 (below the black bar)
In level 2:
- Blue blocks at rows 16-19 (above upper black bar at rows 20-23)
- Blue blocks at rows 24-27 and 36-39 (between the two black bars)
- Blue blocks at rows 44-47 (below lower black bar at rows 40-43)
The figure is at rows 52-57, below the lower black bar. The blue block closest to the figure is at rows 44-47.
In level 1, the winning action was clicking the lower blue (below the bar) 3 times. By analogy, in level 2, I should click the blue block below the lower bar (rows 44-47).
But wait, there's also the white region width to consider. At rows 44-47, the white region is cols 0-7 (8 columns). If each click expands the wall by 4 columns to the left, 2 clicks would fill the white region (8/4 = 2).
Let me try clicking the blue block at rows 44-47 twice.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Level 2: wall on right, 4 blue blocks on left
# Figure at rows 52-57, closest blue block at rows 44-47
# In level 1, clicking lower blue (below bar) expanded wall
# Here, blue below lower bar is at rows 44-47
# White region at rows 44-47 is cols 0-7 (8 cols), so 2 clicks to fill

# Try clicking the blue block at rows 44-47 twice
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': 1}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': 1}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
